title: CO-29 Denial Code: Timely Filing Exceeded — What to Do
meta_description: CO-29 means your claim was filed after the insurer's deadline. Learn when you can still appeal a timely filing denial and what documentation you need.
target_problem: Patient received a CO-29 denial because the claim was submitted past the insurer's timely filing deadline.
body:
CO-29 is issued when a claim is submitted after the insurer's deadline for timely filing. Most commercial insurers require claims to be filed within 90 to 180 days of the service date. Medicare requires claims to be filed within one year.

## When a CO-29 Denial May Be Reversible

Timely filing denials can sometimes be overturned if you can demonstrate:
- The claim was submitted on time but lost or misrouted by the insurer or clearinghouse.
- You have a confirmation number or tracking record of the original submission.
- The late filing was due to retroactive coverage changes outside your control.
- You submitted to the wrong payer first and corrected it within a reasonable time.

## What Documentation You Need

- Original claim submission date and confirmation number (from your provider or clearinghouse)
- Any prior correspondence with the insurer about the claim
- Explanation of any extenuating circumstances

## Steps to Take

1. Request your provider's billing records to confirm the original submission date.
2. File a formal internal appeal with the insurer with the supporting documentation.
3. If the internal appeal is denied, ask your insurer whether external review is available under your plan.

## Disclaimer

This content does not constitute legal or medical advice. MyAdvocate is not a law firm. For questions specific to your situation, consult a qualified attorney or healthcare professional.
